errorBoundary.titulo: Something went wrong in this tool
errorBoundary.mensagemRede: Connection error — the tool could not be loaded. Check your internet and reload.

busca.semResultadosDica: Try different terms or remove the active filters.

lisp-calculator.descricao: Evaluate mathematical expressions using LISP (prefix) notation.

circuito-eletrico.descricao: Calculate currents in electrical circuits using matrix inversion and Kirchhoff's Law.

calculadora-juros.descricao: Calculate simple or compound interest with a period-by-period evolution table.

lispCalculator.erroComparacao: Calculate the first expression before comparing.

complexos.desc: Use the format a+bj ("j" represents the imaginary unit). Some valid examples:

funcoes.raiz: raiz(x) → square root of x (works with negatives)

variaveis.desc: Any letter that is not conj, raiz or j is treated as a variable — a field to fill in the value appears automatically before calculating. Example: a + b * 2.

output.tokens: tokens — the expression broken into pieces
output.posfixa: postfix — internal order used to calculate
output.lisp: lisp — the same expression in prefix notation, e.g.: (+ 3 4j)

comparacao.desc: The "compare with another expression" button checks if two expressions have the same structure (same operators in the same order) — not whether they give the same numerical result.

erros.operadorSemOperandos: Malformed expression: not enough operands for operator
erros.zeroPotenciaNegativa: Cannot raise zero to a negative power

matrixCircuit.subtitulo: Calculate the currents of a 3-mesh circuit using Kirchhoff's Law

erros.camposVazios: Fill in all fields before calculating.
erros.numerosInvalidos: One or more values are not valid numbers.
erros.semSolucao: Malformed circuit (system has no unique solution).

oQueE.desc: Solves a system of 3 linear equations (Kirchhoff's Voltage Law) using 3x3 matrix inversion, finding the electrical current in each mesh of a circuit.

comoPreencher.desc: For each mesh, enter the three resistances (R) that relate that mesh to currents I1, I2 and I3, and the applied voltage (V). Resistance values must be greater than zero.

resultado.desc: Each current (I1, I2, I3) can be positive or negative: positive means the current flows in the initially assumed direction, negative indicates the opposite direction. The verification substitutes the results back into the original equations to confirm calculation accuracy.

interestCalculator.subtitulo: Calculate simple or compound interest and see the period-by-period evolution

erros.camposVazios: Fill in all fields before calculating.
erros.numerosInvalidos: One or more values are not valid numbers.
erros.valoresPositivos: Capital, rate and periods must be greater than zero.
erros.periodosInvalidos: The number of periods must be an integer and at most 600.

ajuda.titulo: How to use the interest calculator

tipos.desc: Simple interest grows linearly — the interest for each period is always calculated on the initial capital. Compound interest grows exponentially — each period's interest is calculated on the accumulated amount (interest on interest).

campos.desc: Enter the initial capital (invested amount), the interest rate per period (in %) and the number of periods. Select the unit (month or year) — when switching, periods are automatically converted to keep the equivalent duration.

taxa.desc: Below the result, the calculator shows the equivalent rate in the other unit. For compound interest, the conversion uses the correct exponential formula — for example, 2% per month is not 24% per year, but approximately 26.82% per year.

tabela.desc: Shows the amount and accumulated interest at the end of each period, allowing you to track the growth of the investment over time.
